scope.objective: Obtain root shell on 10.129.8.218 via Samba CVE-2007-2447 and capture both user.txt and root.txt flags
stop_conditions: root shell obtained and both flags captured

primary_path: Samba 3.0.20 CVE-2007-2447 — username map script command injection via shell metacharacters in SMB username field. Pre-auth, runs as root. Use smbclient or Python PoC with backtick/nohup reverse shell in username.
backup_path: vsftpd 2.3.4 CVE-2011-2523 — send username containing ':)' to trigger backdoor on port 6200. If that fails, distccd CVE-2004-2687 on port 3632 for user shell (nmap NSE script or Metasploit).

vulnerability_primitive.primitive: Unsanitized username string passed directly to /bin/sh via MS-RPC calls in Samba smbd
delivery_forms: smbclient logon command with injected username
Direct MS-RPC SamrChangePassword() call via impacket
vulnerability_primitive.defenses_observed: None — pre-authentication, no input sanitization observed

process_model.critical_note: Exploit fires a reverse shell — start listener BEFORE triggering the injection

notes: This is a trivial exploit. Start listener, inject reverse shell payload into SMB username via smbclient or Python, catch root shell. Capture /root/root.txt and /home/*/user.txt. Should complete in 1-3 turns.
